title: Lithocardium paniculare
taxonomy: Lithocardium paniculare is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This classification makes it a member of the order Gentianales. Further classification places Lithocardium paniculare within the genus Lithocardium, which is a monotypic genus, containing only this species. The family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family, comprises over 2,000 species of plants. As a member of this family, Lithocardium paniculare shares characteristics with other species within the Apocynaceae.
uses: Lithocardium paniculare is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its leaves and roots are used to prepare infusions and decoctions that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocardium paniculare is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. However, more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential side effects.
